slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 7 - Computer Science
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: If a research scenario or project requires: **logic gate reduction optimization**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of logic gate reduction optimization, you should select **Boolean Algebra Minimization**.

slides.question: Q2: If a research scenario or project requires: **graphical boolean simplification table**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of graphical boolean simplification table, you should select **Karnaugh Map (K-map)**.

slides.question: Q3: If a research scenario or project requires: **many-to-one signal selector**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of many-to-one signal selector, you should select **Multiplexer (MUX)**.

slides.question: Q4: If a research scenario or project requires: **one-to-many signal router**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of one-to-many signal router, you should select **Demultiplexer (DEMUX)**.

slides.question: Q5: If a research scenario or project requires: **three-input binary addition circuit**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of three-input binary addition circuit, you should select **Full Adder**.

slides.question: Q6: If a research scenario or project requires: **clock-driven sequential memory components**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of clock-driven sequential memory components, you should select **Flip-Flops**.

slides.question: Q7: If a research scenario or project requires: **simultaneously-clocked state counter**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of simultaneously-clocked state counter, you should select **Synchronous Counter**.

slides.question: Q8: If a research scenario or project requires: **binary-to-unary line converter**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of binary-to-unary line converter, you should select **Decoder**.

slides.question: Q9: If a research scenario or project requires: **shared instruction-data bus architecture**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of shared instruction-data bus architecture, you should select **Von Neumann Architecture**.

slides.question: Q10: If a research scenario or project requires: **multi-level fast memory staging**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of multi-level fast memory staging, you should select **Cache Memory Hierarchy**.

slides.question: Q11: If a research scenario or project requires: **lightweight shared-memory execution unit**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of lightweight shared-memory execution unit, you should select **Thread**.

slides.question: Q12: If a research scenario or project requires: **single-thread resource lock**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single-thread resource lock, you should select **Mutex**.

slides.question: Q13: If a research scenario or project requires: **variable-based process signaling**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of variable-based process signaling, you should select **Counting Semaphore**.

slides.question: Q14: If a research scenario or project requires: **cpu state saving-restoring overhead**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of cpu state saving-restoring overhead, you should select **Context Switching**.

slides.question: Q15: If a research scenario or project requires: **isolated process communication mechanisms**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of isolated process communication mechanisms, you should select **Inter-Process Communication (IPC)**.

slides.question: Q16: If a research scenario or project requires: **speed-matching data storage buffer**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of speed-matching data storage buffer, you should select **I/O Buffering**.

slides.question: Q17: If a research scenario or project requires: **hard drive head seek optimization**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of hard drive head seek optimization, you should select **Disk Scheduling**.

slides.question: Q18: If a research scenario or project requires: **physical disk block mapping strategies**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of physical disk block mapping strategies, you should select **File Allocation Methods**.

slides.question: Q19: If a research scenario or project requires: **privileged kernel service requests interface**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of privileged kernel service requests interface, you should select **System Calls**.

slides.question: Q20: If a research scenario or project requires: **redundancy-performance disk array configurations**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of redundancy-performance disk array configurations, you should select **RAID Levels**.

slides.question: Q21: If a research scenario or project requires: **asymptotic run-time upper bound indicator**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of asymptotic run-time upper bound indicator, you should select **Big O Complexity**.

slides.question: Q22: If a research scenario or project requires: **LIFO element storage collection**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of LIFO element storage collection, you should select **Stack Data Structure**.

slides.question: Q23: If a research scenario or project requires: **FIFO element storage collection**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of FIFO element storage collection, you should select **Queue Data Structure**.

slides.question: Q24: If a research scenario or project requires: **one-way pointer node sequence**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of one-way pointer node sequence, you should select **Singly Linked List**.

slides.question: Q25: If a research scenario or project requires: **two-way pointer node sequence**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of two-way pointer node sequence, you should select **Doubly Linked List**.

slides.question: Q26: If a research scenario or project requires: **O(n log n) stable comparison sort**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of O(n log n) stable comparison sort, you should select **Merge Sort**.

slides.question: Q27: If a research scenario or project requires: **O(log n) sorted array search**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of O(log n) sorted array search, you should select **Binary Search**.

slides.question: Q28: If a research scenario or project requires: **active subroutine memory tracker**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of active subroutine memory tracker, you should select **Call Stack**.

slides.question: Q29: If a research scenario or project requires: **retrieving value from pointer address**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of retrieving value from pointer address, you should select **Pointer dereferencing**.

slides.question: Q30: If a research scenario or project requires: **single interface multi-implementation capability**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single interface multi-implementation capability, you should select **Polymorphism**.

slides.question: Q31: If a research scenario or project requires: **virtual table query abstraction**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of virtual table query abstraction, you should select **Database View**.

slides.question: Q32: If a research scenario or project requires: **non-relational schema-less storage**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of non-relational schema-less storage, you should select **NoSQL Database**.

slides.question: Q33: If a research scenario or project requires: **logical database structure visualization**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of logical database structure visualization, you should select **ER Modeling**.

slides.question: Q34: If a research scenario or project requires: **reverting uncommitted transactional modifications**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reverting uncommitted transactional modifications, you should select **Transaction Rollback**.

slides.question: Q35: If a research scenario or project requires: **concurrent write overwrite anomaly**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of concurrent write overwrite anomaly, you should select **Lost Update Anomaly**.

slides.question: Q36: If a research scenario or project requires: **reading uncommitted transaction modifications**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reading uncommitted transaction modifications, you should select **Dirty Read**.

slides.question: Q37: If a research scenario or project requires: **reading committed concurrent modifications**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reading committed concurrent modifications, you should select **Non-Repeatable Read**.

slides.question: Q38: If a research scenario or project requires: **reading committed concurrent insertions**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reading committed concurrent insertions, you should select **Phantom Read**.

slides.question: Q39: If a research scenario or project requires: **efficient execution plan selector**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of efficient execution plan selector, you should select **Query Optimizer**.

slides.question: Q40: If a research scenario or project requires: **automatic event-driven code block**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of automatic event-driven code block, you should select **Database Trigger**.

slides.question: Q41: If a research scenario or project requires: **flexible iterative development paradigm**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of flexible iterative development paradigm, you should select **Agile Methodology**.

slides.question: Q42: If a research scenario or project requires: **reusable architectural solution blueprint**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of reusable architectural solution blueprint, you should select **Design Pattern**.

slides.question: Q43: If a research scenario or project requires: **isolated component validation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of isolated component validation, you should select **Unit Testing**.

slides.question: Q44: If a research scenario or project requires: **distributed source control management**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of distributed source control management, you should select **Git Version Control**.

slides.question: Q45: If a research scenario or project requires: **automated deployment automation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of automated deployment automation, you should select **CI/CD Pipeline**.

slides.question: Q46: If a research scenario or project requires: **functional specification validation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of functional specification validation, you should select **Black-box Testing**.

slides.question: Q47: If a research scenario or project requires: **internal code path validation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of internal code path validation, you should select **White-box Testing**.

slides.question: Q48: If a research scenario or project requires: **high-level structural system organization**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of high-level structural system organization, you should select **Software Architecture**.

slides.question: Q49: If a research scenario or project requires: **sprint-based team collaboration**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of sprint-based team collaboration, you should select **Scrum Framework**.

slides.question: Q50: If a research scenario or project requires: **single module functional focus score**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single module functional focus score, you should select **Cohesion**.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 7. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Computer Science Mock Exam 7.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Computer Science questions!
